original_hypothesis.title: Unbounded `holders` growth via 0-amount minting to approved addresses can create long-term distribution gas DoS
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The owner can mint 0 tokens to many approved addresses; `_beforeTokenTransfer` will treat each as a new holder (balanceOf(to)==0) and push them into `holders`. This increases distribution iteration costs without increasing supply meaningfully, making distributions expensive and potentially infeasible.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Although owner-only, this is an easy operational mistake or insider-risk that can materially degrade distribution reliability. It’s a code defect in holder-tracking (treating 0-amount mints as new holders) that should be validated and mitigated operationally.
deployment_assumptions: Owner uses automated minting scripts that may mint 0 amounts (buggy airdrop tooling) or bulk-approves many holders before minting meaningful balances
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: No production minting tooling provided; Hardhat `transferERC20ToHolders` mints nonzero amounts, but does not prevent 0 amounts.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _beforeTokenTransfer(): if balanceOf(to)==0 then holders.push(to); no guard for amount==0; called on mint (from==address(0))

preconditions: Owner has approved many addresses in HolderAllowlist
Owner performs mint(account, 0) repeatedly to distinct accounts
Distributions are expected to process all holders

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Holder tracking does not enforce positive balances for inclusion and ignores `amount`.

operational_questions: Do operational scripts ever mint zero amounts (edge cases, rounding, retries)?
Is there any holder cleanup procedure for zero-balance holders?
Can a large holders set cause distribution to become infeasible on the target chain?
validation_requirements: Test: approve N addresses, mint 0 to each, confirm holders length grows and distribution gas increases
Assess if `_afterTokenTransfer` removal can ever remove these accounts (it only removes `from` on transfers/burns)

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Owner mints 0 to each, populating `holders` with zero-balance accounts.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Distribution loops waste gas over many zero-balance holders; completion requires many calls.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Higher distribution costs; longer LockedForDistribution windows; potential inability to complete distributions
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High (no batch cleanup of zero-balance holders)
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Yield distribution cadence and ERC20 usability during distributions

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Many Mint events with amount=0 (if emitted by ERC20)
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Growing holders without corresponding totalSupply growth

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
